checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=5777619.03 (2010-12-31) growing to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=15158733.98 (2012-12-31), a ~162% increase
Goodwill=61360040.5 (2010-12-31) jumping to Goodwill=94864277.62 (2011-12-31), a ~55% increase in one year vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of 13% (321776172.03 FY2011 vs 284705944.4 FY2010)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 balance sheet / income statement, comparative 2010/2011

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2012, comparative FY2011 income statement and cash flow statement
10-K FY2012 income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=184092027.98 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=220472275.15 (FY2009) = 83.5% gross margin, declining to GrossProfit=277924609.18 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=369252662.48 (FY2012) = 75.3% gross margin
CostOfGoodsSold=36380247.16 (FY2009) growing to CostOfGoodsSold=91328053.3 (FY2012), COGS growing faster (+151%) than SalesRevenueGoodsNet (+67% from 220472275.15 to 369252662.48)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss swings sharply: 46604936.17 (FY2010) to -41582783.24 (FY2011) to 1871698.53 (FY2012), including a single-quarter loss of NetIncomeLoss=-40215324.11 (Q3 2011), while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew steadily each year (284705944.4, 321776172.03, 369252662.48)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 comparative annual and quarterly income statement data

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2478333.64 (2009-12-31) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=34803829.32 (ratio 7.1%), declining to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1655000.61 (2012-12-31) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=52958828.79 (ratio 3.1%)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 and prior 10-K FY2009 balance sheet notes

evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=15471874.38 (2010-12-31) declining to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12430364.27 (2012-12-31) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew from 284705944.4 (FY2010) to 369252662.48 (FY2012)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2010-02-26 followed shortly by a 10-K/A filed 2010-05-26
Multiple UPLOAD and CORRESP filings (SEC comment-letter correspondence) clustered in 2010-07-30 through 2010-10-12, and again 2012-09-14 through 2012-10-18

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly points from FY2007 through FY2012 across Assets, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, CashFlow, Receivables, Inventory, Goodwill and related concepts, sourced from multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings (e.g., accession 0000950123-11-018857, 0001193125-12-078937, 0001445305-13-000348)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Bad-debt allowance and accrued liability reserves appear to have been under-provisioned relative to receivables/revenue growth (allowance/AR ratio fell from ~7.1% in 2009 to ~3.1% in 2012; accrued liabilities fell in absolute terms even as revenue grew ~30%). Under-reserving of receivables and accrued expenses would understate expenses and overstate net income and net receivables in the relevant periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12430364.27 (2012-12-31) vs AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=15471874.38 (2010-12-31) despite SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth from 284705944.4 to 369252662.48

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A large, possibly non-recurring/non-cash charge in Q3 2011 (NetIncomeLoss=-40215324.11) drove a full-year net loss (-41582783.24) even as operating cash flow remained strongly positive (37484573.11), and the pattern recurred in FY2012 (NetIncomeLoss=1871698.53 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=77440931.29). This large and persistent NI/CFO gap, occurring alongside a major Goodwill increase (61360040.5 to 94864277.62 in FY2011, consistent with an acquisition), is consistent with either aggressive non-cash impairment/purchase-accounting charges depressing reported earnings while cash generation remained robust, or conversely with earnings being managed downward via one-time charges to smooth future periods.

Rapid growth in 'soft' non-current other assets (5777619.03 in 2010 to 15158733.98 in 2012, +162%) far outpacing revenue growth (~30% over same period), combined with a steady decline in gross margin (83.5% in FY2009 to 75.3% in FY2012) driven by COGS rising faster than revenue, could indicate capitalization of costs that should be expensed or delayed recognition of cost increases, potentially overstating non-current assets and near-term profitability.

top_signals: Persistent and widening divergence between NetIncomeLoss and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities in FY2011 (-41.6M NI vs +37.5M CFO) and FY2012 (1.9M NI vs 77.4M CFO)
Declining allowance-for-doubtful-accounts coverage ratio (from ~7.1% of AR in 2009 to ~3.1% in 2012) despite AR growth
Accrued liabilities shrinking (15.47M to 12.43M, 2010-2012) despite ~30% revenue growth over the same period
Steady gross margin erosion (83.5% to 75.3%, FY2009-FY2012) with COGS growing faster than revenue
Rapid Goodwill and Other Assets Noncurrent growth outpacing revenue, alongside a 10-K/A amendment and multiple SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD filings in 2010 and 2012 suggesting regulatory scrutiny
